general: 1. What was your very first impression of me?
2. When did you first realise you were attracted to me?
3. What do you remember most clearly about our first date?
4. What is one small thing I do that makes you feel loved?
5. What do you think other people notice about us as a couple?
6. How would you describe our relationship to someone who has never met us?
7. What part of our daily routine together do you secretly treasure?
8. When do you feel the safest with me?
9. What is one fear about relationships you had before us that feels smaller now?
10. What does commitment mean to you in our context?
11. What is one way we communicate well?
12. What is one way we can communicate better?
13. When was a time I made you feel really seen?
14. What is something you wish I would ask you more often?
15. What kind of support from me means the most to you when you are stressed?
16. What are three words that describe how you feel when we are together?
17. What is something I do that you think I do not realise you appreciate?
18. How do you know when I am not okay, even if I say I am fine?
19. What is one boundary that is important to you in our relationship?
20. What have you learned about love from being with me?
21. How has being with me changed the way you see yourself?
22. What did you expect our relationship to be like at the start, and how is it different now?
23. What is one thing you admire about how I handle challenges?
24. What is one thing you admire about how I treat other people?
25. Which local place in Singapore feels symbolic of our relationship, and why?
26. If our relationship were a Singapore food, what would it be and why?
27. When did you last feel proud of us as a couple?
28. What is something we do differently from other couples that you like?
29. What is a conversation we had that you still think about?
30. What part of my personality did you underestimate at first?
31. How do you think we handle conflict as a couple?
32. What is one thing I do in arguments that helps you feel safe?
33. What is one thing I do in arguments that makes it harder for you to open up?
34. When was a time we overcame something difficult together?
35. What is one topic that is hard for you to bring up with me, and why?
36. What would make difficult conversations feel easier between us?
37. What is one expectation about relationships you grew up with that you are rethinking with me?
38. What does quality time look like to you in Singapore’s busy lifestyle?
39. What is your favourite simple moment we have shared at home?
40. What is your favourite memory of us outside, like at a mall, park, or hawker centre?
41. If we had a free weekend with no plans, how would you love to spend it together?
42. What is one new experience in Singapore you would like us to try as a couple?
43. How do you feel when we talk about the future?
44. Where do you imagine us living together in five years?
45. What kind of home environment do you hope we will build together?
46. What is one value you really want us to pass on if we have children?
47. What is one fear you have about our future that you have not said aloud?
48. What reassures you about our future when you feel uncertain?
49. What is one dream you think we can support each other in achieving?
50. How do you define success for us as a couple?
51. What do you think is our biggest strength as a team?
52. What is something we still need to learn about each other?
53. How do you feel about the way we handle money and expenses together?
54. What is your ideal balance between personal space and together time?
55. What habit of mine would you gently like me to improve, and why?
56. What is one habit of yours you are working on for our relationship?
57. What does romance look like to you now, compared to your younger self?
58. What is the most romantic thing I have done for you so far?
59. What is one everyday gesture that feels romantic to you?
60. How do you feel loved without words?
61. How important are text messages and calls in helping you feel connected day to day?
62. What kind of message from me instantly lifts your mood?
63. When we are apart, what do you miss most about me?
64. What is one song that reminds you of us?
65. If our relationship had a movie title, what would it be?
66. What chapter of our relationship do you think we are in right now, and why?
67. What is something you are still curious about in my past?
68. What part of my childhood do you think shaped who I am with you?
69. How do you feel seeing me with my family or close friends?
70. What is one thing you hope my family or friends appreciate about you?
71. What role do you hope I play in your life during your toughest seasons?
72. When you think about growing old together, what image comes to mind?
73. What scares you most about long-term relationships, and what comforts you most about ours?
74. How do you feel our relationship fits into Singapore’s culture and expectations?
75. Have you ever compared us to another couple? What did you notice?
76. What do you want to be better at for us one year from now?
77. What is one thing you hope never changes between us?
78. What is something about me you think I do not give myself enough credit for?
79. How do you feel when I apologise? What makes an apology feel sincere to you?
80. What is something I did recently that made you feel valued?
81. When do you feel the most connected to me physically or emotionally?
82. What does intimacy mean to you beyond physical touch?
83. How can I better support your mental health or emotional well-being?
84. What is one stress from Singapore life (work, commute, cost of living) that you wish I understood more deeply?
85. How do you feel about how we divide responsibilities like chores or planning?
86. What ritual would you like us to start, like weekly walks, kopi dates, or no-phone dinners?
87. When you imagine our next holiday together, what do you hope it feels like?
88. What is one apology you have never spoken out loud to a past partner that you have learned from now?
89. What is one thing you are afraid to ask me, and why?
90. If we had to restart our relationship from day one, what would you do differently?
91. What made you choose to stay with me during a tough moment instead of walking away?
92. What do you think I need from you more often but rarely ask for?
93. What is one belief about love you are still figuring out?
94. How do you know when our relationship is in a good place?
95. What part of our story so far are you most grateful for?
96. What does loyalty look like to you in real, everyday terms?
97. When was a time you felt I truly had your back?
98. What do you want our relationship to teach the people around us?
99. If you could send a message to our future selves as a couple, what would it say?
100. What is one promise you quietly make to me when you think about our future?
1. What first drew you to me as a friend?
2. What do you think our friendship looked like from the outside when we first met?
3. What is your earliest memory of us hanging out?
4. When did you realise this friendship was different from a normal acquaintance?
5. What is something you appreciate about how I show up as a friend?
6. What kind of friend do you think I am to you in one sentence?
7. What is one moment when you felt really supported by me?
8. What is one moment when you wish I had been there more for you?
9. How do you usually know when I am not okay, even if I say I am fine?
10. What do you think is our strongest shared trait?
11. What is one way we are total opposites, and how does that help our friendship?
12. When was a time we laughed so hard we could not stop?
13. What is your favourite random memory of us in Singapore?
14. Which local spot feels like “our place” and why?
15. What has our friendship taught you about trust?
16. What has our friendship taught you about yourself?
17. What is one thing you think I do not realise you are grateful for?
18. When do you feel closest to me as a friend?
19. What kind of messages from me make you feel cared for?
20. How do you like friends to show support when you are going through a rough time?
21. When was a time you felt misunderstood by your friends in general?
22. How can I be a better listener for you?
23. What is one boundary that is important to you in friendships?
24. What does loyalty in friendship mean to you in practical terms?
25. When did you last feel let down by a friend (not necessarily me), and what did you learn from it?
26. How do you feel when friendships slowly fade out?
27. What makes you decide that a friendship is worth fighting for?
28. What is something you wish more friends would ask you?
29. If our friendship had a theme song, what kind of mood would it have?
30. What kind of energy do you think we bring when we show up to gatherings together?
31. What is one memory of us on public transport (MRT, bus, Grab) that still makes you smile?
32. Which late-night or after-work moment with me stands out most in your mind?
33. How do you think we have changed since we first met?
34. What is one way I have grown that you have noticed?
35. What is one way you hope I see your growth?
36. What do you think keeps our friendship strong in such a busy city?
37. When life gets hectic, what helps you feel our friendship is still solid?
38. What is one thing you want us to do more often together?
39. What is one new activity or place in Singapore you want to explore with me?
40. What kind of friend do you want to be in the next few years?
41. How do you feel about mixing different friend groups together?
42. What is something you were nervous to tell me at first but are glad you did?
43. When was a time we handled a disagreement or awkward moment well?
44. When was a time we did not handle something so well, and what could we learn from it?
45. How comfortable are you being vulnerable with me, and what makes it easier or harder?
46. What do you think I can trust you with that I might not fully realise?
47. What is one secret fear you have about friendships ending?
48. What reassures you that our friendship is stable?
49. What role do you think I play in your life story?
50. What role do you think you play in mine?
51. What is something you imagine us still doing together 10 years from now?
52. If we lost touch for a while, what would you hope stays the same when we reconnect?
53. What kind of crisis would you absolutely call me for?
54. What kind of good news would you be excited to share with me first?
55. What is one way we hype each other up that you love?
56. What is one area you think I underestimate myself in?
57. When do you feel most like your true self around me?
58. What is one thing you worried I might judge you for, but I did not?
59. How do you feel when we drift a bit due to commitments like work or school?
60. What helps you feel reconnected after we have not met for a while?
61. Which festival or holiday memory with friends in Singapore stands out most to you?
62. What is your favourite food memory of us, whether it is prata, mala, or kopi chats?
63. If our friendship were a local dish, what would it be and why?
64. What is something you think we should try at least once, even if it feels slightly out of character?
65. What have you learned about friendship from growing up in Singapore?
66. How did your experiences in school or NS shape the kind of friend you are today?
67. What is one childhood friendship experience that still affects how you handle friendships now?
68. What does “showing up” as a friend mean to you in practical terms?
69. When was the last time you felt lonely, even with people around you?
70. How can friends help with that kind of loneliness?
71. What is one thing you wish our friend group would talk about more honestly?
72. What is one thing you wish we would do less of as a group?
73. If we created a group tradition, what would you want it to be?
74. What kind of trips or staycations do you imagine us going on together?
75. What is a small inside joke or reference between us that still makes you smile?
76. When you think of “home,” do any memories with friends come to mind?
77. What is something you are currently going through that you wish friends understood better?
78. How can I better support your mental health as a friend?
79. What is one thing you admire about how I handle stress or pressure?
80. What is one thing you wish I would not worry so much about?
81. If you could thank me for one specific moment in our friendship, what would it be?
82. What is something you want to apologise for in our friendship, if anything?
83. What kind of honesty do you value most in a friend?
84. Have I ever told you something honest that you really needed to hear?
85. What is something you are afraid to ask your friends in general?
86. What topic do you think we avoid but might help us grow if we talked about it?
87. What is one dream or goal you would like me to hold you accountable for?
88. How would you like me to celebrate your achievements with you?
89. What is one way friendships in Singapore feel unique to you?
90. How do you balance being “busy” with maintaining friendships here?
91. What do you think makes our friendship worth protecting?
92. What is something you hope never changes between us?
93. If we wrote a short chapter about our friendship, what would its title be?
94. What do you hope I remember most about you years from now?
95. What do you hope to remember most about me?
96. What is one belief about friendship you are still figuring out?
97. What is one thing you hope our friendship teaches others who see it?
98. If this were the last time we played this game, what would you want to say to me?
99. What question do you wish I would ask you right now?
100. What is one promise you want to make to yourself about how you will show up in our friendship?
1. What is one of your earliest memories of our family?
2. How would you describe our family to someone who has never met us?
3. What is a typical family moment that feels very “us”?
4. What is one thing our family does that you secretly cherish?
5. What does “home” mean to you when you think about our family?
6. What family value do you think shaped you the most growing up?
7. What is one thing you think our family is really good at?
8. What is one thing you wish our family did better?
9. When do you feel most connected to the family?
10. When do you feel the most distant from the family?
11. What is a family tradition you never want to lose?
12. What is a new tradition you wish we would start?
13. Which local festival or holiday means the most to you, and why?
14. What is your favourite memory of celebrating Chinese New Year, Hari Raya, Deepavali, or Christmas with family?
15. What food immediately reminds you of family time?
16. Which hawker centre, kopitiam, or restaurant holds special family memories for you?
17. What is one small family habit that outsiders might not understand but you love?
18. What is something our family used to do that you miss?
19. Who in the family makes you feel especially safe, and how?
20. Who in the family has challenged you the most, and what did you learn from it?
21. What is something you wish you could say to the family but have not yet?
22. What is a topic you find hard to bring up with family?
23. What would make conversations in our family feel more open and honest?
24. How did the way we handle conflict at home shape how you handle conflict now?
25. What is a time when you felt misunderstood by the family?
26. What is a time when you felt deeply understood by the family?
27. What is one thing you admire about how our family has handled a tough situation?
28. What is one moment that made you feel especially proud of our family?
29. How have you seen our family change over the years?
30. What do you think has stayed the same about our family despite all the changes?
31. What was a typical school day like for you growing up in this family?
32. How did our family respond to your studies, exams, or results when you were younger?
33. What messages about success or failure did you pick up from our family?
34. How has that affected the way you see yourself now?
35. What did you learn about money from our family?
36. What did you learn about love from our family?
37. What did you learn about responsibility from our family?
38. What do you wish our family had talked more openly about when you were younger?
39. What are you grateful that our family did talk about?
40. When you think of childhood comfort, what family scene appears in your mind?
41. What is a memory of a family outing in Singapore that still makes you smile?
42. What is a memory of a family trip, overseas or local, that stands out to you?
43. If you had to choose one photo that represents our family, what would it show?
44. Who in the family do you wish you knew better, and why?
45. What would you like to ask an older family member about their younger days?
46. What do you think the younger generation in our family does not fully understand about the older generation?
47. What do you think the older generation does not fully understand about the younger generation?
48. What could each generation learn from the other in our family?
49. How do you feel about the way we show affection in our family?
50. What kind of affection or affirmation means the most to you from family members?
51. What is something you appreciate about how our family handled difficult times like illness, loss, or financial stress?
52. What would you change about how we handle crises together?
53. When was a time a family member’s support really mattered to you?
54. When was a time you wish the family had shown up more for you?
55. How do you think our family deals with emotions such as sadness or anger?
56. What would make it safer to talk about those emotions at home?
57. What is a story about our family you hope will be passed on to future generations?
58. What is a story about our family you think we rarely talk about but should?
59. How has growing up in Singapore shaped our family’s habits and priorities?
60. What do you think is uniquely “Singaporean” about our family life?
61. How has housing, school, or work in Singapore affected our family time together?
62. What does a perfect slow weekend with family look like to you?
63. What is one small change that could make everyday family life feel more peaceful?
64. What do you think each of us brings to the family that is special?
65. What do you think you personally contribute to the family?
66. What do you want the family to understand better about who you are now?
67. How different is the person you are at home from the person you are outside?
68. What would help you feel more fully yourself at home?
69. What is one thing you admire about how a family member lives their life?
70. What is one life choice of a family member that made you think differently about your own path?
71. How do you feel when the family compares you with others, whether relatives or friends?
72. What kind of comparisons, if any, feel helpful instead of hurtful?
73. What do you wish our family would celebrate more, besides grades, promotions, or milestones?
74. What is something small about you that you would love the family to notice and affirm?
75. How do you imagine our family gatherings will look 10 years from now?
76. What traditions do you hope will still be around then?
77. What new traditions do you hope we start by then?
78. How do you feel about the idea of caring for older family members in the future?
79. What kind of support do you hope the family will offer one another as everyone ages?
80. What do you think our family needs more of right now?
81. What do you think our family needs less of right now?
82. What personal boundary with family are you learning to protect?
83. What boundary do you appreciate others respecting?
84. What role does faith, culture, or belief play in our family, if any?
85. How has that shaped your outlook on life?
86. If you could rewrite one family moment, what would you change, if anything?
87. If you could relive one family moment exactly as it was, which would you choose?
88. What apology do you wish you could receive from the family or a family member?
89. What apology do you feel ready to offer to the family or someone in it?
90. What is one thing you are proud of surviving or overcoming as part of this family?
91. What do you want the next generation in our family to know about where we came from?
92. What do you want them to do differently from us?
93. What do you hope they keep exactly the same?
94. What is one way you would like us to speak to each other more kindly at home?
95. What does support from family look like to you during stressful exams, work, or life events?
96. What is a dream or goal you have that you would love the family to stand behind?
97. How can we as a family make it easier to ask for help?
98. What do you think makes our family worth holding on to through hard times?
99. If you could write a letter to the whole family, what is one line you would definitely include?
100. What kind of family story do you hope we will be telling about ourselves in the future?
